sanskrit:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते॥
transliteration:
yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dhanañjaya |
siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṃ yoga ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Established in yoga / perform / actions / attachment / having abandoned / O Dhanañjaya / in success and failure / equipoised / having become / equanimity / yoga / is called
contextual_meaning: Krishna instructs Arjuna to act from a state of inner balance, relinquishing anxious preoccupation with outcomes; equanimity toward gain or loss is the hallmark of integrated action.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative performance anxiety shifting toward process-focused engagement; egoic attachment to success/failure being replaced by intrinsic valuing of right action.
trigger_scenario: A student awaiting exam results compulsively refreshes the grade portal, unable to enjoy learning or sleep; the verse reframes the task as studying with full presence while releasing obsessive score-tracking.
anchor_text: Work steadily, unattached to success or failure, keeping your mind calm and even.
